---
title: "LRPGF11: Factors associated with gains in nonword reading (N) — off-floor"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by a LLM-based AI tool (Claude Code/Opus 4.8).
:::

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRPGF11 is the **gain-factors** model for nonword reading (`nonword`).

::: {.callout-important title="Floored outcome - off-floor likelihood"}

Nonword reading (6 items) is **heavily floored** (most period post-scores are zero; the at-floor rate runs 72% → 40% across t1–t4), so a graded Beta-Binomial gain would be driven by a few dispersed tail values rather than the factor contrasts. Following the suite's floor rule, the likelihood is a **Bernoulli on the binary off-floor indicator** (period post $> 0$): the linear predictor models the **log-odds of being off the floor at post** — pooling zero→positive movement, staying off the floor, and return-to-floor together — **not** the log-odds of "coming off the floor". `beta_trt` is the off-floor log-odds of being on intervention, and its items-scale marginal collapses to the **off-floor risk difference** (reported against the 10 pp δ adopted for floored outcomes).

:::

::: {.callout-important title="Causal labelling (DAG guardrails)"}

Only the randomised on-intervention term is causal. Under the revised DAG (`dag/dag-language-reading.dagitty`, revised 2026-07-10) the **period-1 average marginal effect** — the genuinely randomised, all-untreated-baseline transition — is the ITT-anchor estimand; `beta_trt` itself is only the on-intervention log-odds contrast, not the ITT effect. Own baseline, age, ability (blocks), the upstream code skills letter sounds (L) and blending (B), and the speech and phonological-memory confounders (SP = `deapp_c`, RW = `erbto`, each with a missing-indicator; hearing HS is not a parent of nonword reading) are **adjusted associations**: the child random intercept is a partial, shrunken stand-in for between-child heterogeneity and does **not** control latent general ability, so these internal slopes stay descriptive - "associated with", never "drives". This is the causal-term counterpart of the mech-072/172 L/B→N association. Sessions/dose are never conditioned on (collider); SES is excluded (non-DAG / redundant).

:::

## Model

$$
(N_{\text{post}} > 0) \sim \operatorname{Bernoulli}(\operatorname{expit}(\eta)),\quad
\eta = \alpha + \alpha_{\text{period}} + \beta_{\text{trt}}\text{OnIntervention}
  + \gamma_{\text{own}}\operatorname{logit}_*(N_{\text{pre}}) + \gamma_A A_{\text{std}}
  + \gamma_{\text{ability}}z(\text{blocks}) + \sum_{s\in\{L,B\}}\gamma_s\operatorname{logit}_*(s_{\text{pre}})
  + \sum_{c}\gamma_c\,z(c)
  + \textstyle\sum\text{interactions} + u_{\text{child}}.
$$

Here $\sum_{c}\gamma_c\,z(c)$ are the standardised speech and phonological-memory confounders (SP = `deapp_c`, RW = `erbto`), each entered with a missing-indicator. No Beta-Binomial concentration (`kappa`) under the off-floor Bernoulli. Priors follow the shared template in `priors.py`.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_results_factors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
